description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a compact fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by wooden struts. The wings and tail surfaces are painted a light cream color with prominent red roundels, suggesting a historical military or training role. The nose features a radial engine with a wooden propeller, and the engine cowling is metallic gray. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a large horizontal stabilizer, both matching the wing color scheme. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with visible control elements. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with exposed structural elements and a simple, functional aesthetic.
description: A vintage cream-colored biplane with red roundels, wooden struts, and a radial engine with a wooden propeller. Features an open cockpit and classic early aviation design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radial engine, wooden propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with red roundels.
description_view_1: Angled top-side view highlighting the upper and lower wings, open cockpit, and the tail's large horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, including the single vertical stabilizer and wide horizontal stabilizer, as well as the wing structure.
description_view_3: Angled top-side view from the opposite side, showing the propeller, cockpit, and the arrangement of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a large horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with fixed main gear and a tail skid or small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
